data.body: Terraform applied (2026-07-10 ~17:00 UTC, account 896586841071): targeted apply of exactly the two alarm resources (drift-scoped: full plan contained unrelated pre-existing drift — SSM secret params, SNS subscription, aws_instance — deliberately NOT applied). Result: aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm.deploy_errors updated in-place (period 300->900, evals 2->4, datapoints_to_alarm 0->2) + aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm.root_disk_pressure created (>85%, 2-of-3 x 300s). describe-alarms confirms both shapes. Metric verification uncovered TWO blockers, both remediated on-box: (1) /usr/local/bin/rebar-observability.sh was a STALE Jun 30 copy (7KB, no root_disk/4c/4d sections) — autodeploy does not refresh installer-materialized host scripts; re-ran install-observability.sh from /opt/rebar (now 16KB, timer re-enabled). (2) observability.sh's final statement is a false '[ 0 -gt 0 ]' test with no trailing exit 0, so every run exits 1 and systemd marks the unit failed (metrics still publish). CloudWatch now shows rebar/host:root_disk_used_percent = 29.0 (Maximum, 16:58 UTC datapoint); alarm will move INSUFFICIENT_DATA->OK as datapoints accrue. Cadence math for AC1: at BACKOFF_CAP=900s, one AUTODEPLOY_ERROR per 15min => every 900s period has Sum>=1 => 2-of-4 latches within 30-60min. Both discoveries filed as bugs linked discovered_from this ticket.
